title: GUARDIANSHIP/CONSERVATORSHIP PROGRAMS - 211info
text: SENIOR CITIZENS COUNCIL OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY GUARDIANSHIP/CONSERVATORSHIP PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides guardianship, conservatorship, family support, case management, and advocacy services through the following programs: Guardianship/Conservatorship Program: Provides guardianship and conservatorship services for those who are not capable of making appropriate decisions about their well-being and who have no family or friends willing or able to accept such responsibilities. If all other appropriate alternatives have been tried, agency can serve as the Guardian and/or Conservator for the person. Guardianship Diversion Program: Helps ensure that all possible alternatives are explored before the step of adjudication is taken. Program offers client assessment, personal support and encouragement to clients and/or families, short-term counseling, placement counseling, case monitoring, outreach, community education about alternatives to guardianship, and information/referral to community resources. 812 7th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, and some adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 657-1366 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 657-1366 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for application information or to make a referral FEES: Guardianship/Conservatorship: $100 per hour, charged in 15-minute increments. Services are provided regardless of ability to pay, as long as funding is available. Guardianship Diversion: $35 per hour, charged in 15-minute increments. Services are provided regardless of ability to pay, as long as funding is available. DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Clackamas County Services: Guardianship Assistance Private Guardianship/Conservatorship Programs If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SENIOR CITIZENS COUNCIL OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY
